# MKUltraman — Digital Infrastructure for SMEs in Japan

> Matt Ketchum is a Tokyo-based digital infrastructure consultant with 10+ years
> in the Japan market. He audits, rebuilds, and manages the technology
> stacks of small and medium enterprises across Japan, specializing in
> eliminating tool sprawl, fixing broken workflows, and building measurable
> operations. He has worked with 40+ SMEs and averages a 30% reduction in
> tool spend per engagement.

## Services

- Stack Audit (one-time): Maps full digital infrastructure, identifies
  waste and broken systems, delivers a prioritized action plan.
  Most clients cut 30% of tool spend within 60 days.
- Infrastructure Build (scoped project): Redesigns and rebuilds the
  digital stack with integrated workflows, documentation, and team training.
- Ongoing Management (monthly retainer): Manages all digital infrastructure
  on an ongoing basis — vendors, tools, monitoring, and optimization.

## About

Matt Ketchum (MKUltraman) operates under the principle that most SME problems are
structural, not people problems. Based in Tokyo. Works with a small client
roster at a time to go deep. Industries served: F&B, media, professional
services, events, retail.

## Blog

73 articles covering digital transformation in Japan, SME strategy,
technology adoption failures, Japan business culture, and market analysis.
URL: https://mkultraman.com/blog/

## Key Pages

- Homepage: https://mkultraman.com/
- Blog index: https://mkultraman.com/blog/
- Contact/Inquiry: https://mkultraman.com/#inquiry

## Network

MKUltraman is the hub of the Ultra Guild — a network of 10 independent
sites spanning music, food, travel, art, and storytelling across Japan:
- https://japanesejesus.com
- https://wildmeatjapan.com
- https://musicinjapan.com
- https://japancraftbeer.com
- https://akiyaz.io
- https://kaalamusic.com
- https://heatherdobbin.com
- https://thedelphinetwork.com
- https://thomsmithart.com
